questions.question: The London Heathrow (EGLL) airport runway 09R/27L is 12,008 feet long and the runway 09L/27R is 12,799 feet long. What is the difference in length in meters?
If there is a 55 gallon barrel 60% full with oil (Barrel A), and a 55 gallon barrel 80% full with AVGAS (Barrel B), which is heavier and what is the difference in weight between to the two barrels?
Departing from KMMH (Mammoth Lakes, CA, elevation 7135 feet) on a summer day (83°F) with an atmospheric pressure of 29.92", what would the Density Altitude be?
Referring to question 3, if you were to returned to a winter day (8°F) with an atmospheric pressure of 30.20", how much lower is the Density Altitude?
questions.context: Referenced question 3: Departing from KMMH (Mammoth Lakes, CA, elevation 7135 feet) on a summer day (83°F) with an atmospheric pressure of 29.92", what would the Density Altitude be?

questions.question: After flying for 2 hours 37 minutes at a ground speed of 190 knots, how far have you flown?
While flying at a pressure altitude of 8500 feet, with a IAS of 220 km/hr, and an OAT of -1°C, what would your TAS be?
1 (Referring to Question #6) With a forecasted winds aloft of 340° at 29 kts and on a true course of 220°, what is your ground speed?
questions.context: Referenced question 6: While flying at a pressure altitude of 8500 feet, with a IAS of 220 km/hr, and an OAT of -1°C, what would your TAS be?

questions.question: If the winds turn out to be 265° at 36 kts, how much longer your flight take?
questions.context: Referenced question 6: While flying at a pressure altitude of 8500 feet, with a IAS of 220 km/hr, and an OAT of -1°C, what would your TAS be? [[DIVIDER]] Referenced question 7: 1 (Referring to Question #6) With a forecasted winds aloft of 340° at 29 kts and on a true course of 220°, what is your ground speed? [[DIVIDER]] (Referring to Question #7) The distance to your destination is 173 NM.

questions.question: While climbing at 860 Ft/NM with a rate of climb of 2330 FPM, what is the ground speed?

Your loaded aircraft weighs 2900 pounds with a CG of 75 inches. Your maximum takeoff weight is 2800 pounds and the aft limit of the CG envelope is 73 inches. You decide to remove a portion of the 250 pounds of cargo at a station of 100 inches to bring the aircraft back into the allowable range. How much will you need to remove?
There are two barrels (weighing the same), one filled with AVGAS and one filled with oil. If barrel one is filled with 20 US Gallons of AVGAS, how many liters of oil are in barrel two?

Departing QRS airport, there is an obstacle 0.65 NM beyond the point of rotation. This obstacle is 212 feet tall and the pilot wishes to clear it by 50 feet. If the ground speed will be 79 knots, what is the required rate of climb to clear the obstacle as desired?
At a ground speed of 283 km/hr, how many NM are covered in 8 minutes and 6 seconds? _______________________________ Nautical Miles (to the tenth of a nautical mile)

A flight departs Los Angeles International (LAX) to Medford, OR (MFR), 448 NM apart. After flying 200 NM, this pilot discovers that the aircraft is 19.8 NM right of course. If the original heading is 003°, what is the new heading to parallel your course?
(Referring Question #14) If the pilot wishes to rejoin the original course in 50 NM, what is the new heading to rejoin the course?
questions.context: Referenced question 14: A flight departs Los Angeles International (LAX) to Medford, OR (MFR), 448 NM apart. After flying 200 NM, this pilot discovers that the aircraft is 19.8 NM right of course. If the original heading is 003°, what is the new heading to parallel your course?

questions.question: While flying on a true course of 328°, with winds aloft forecasted to be 300° at 28 kts, and a TAS of 158 kts, what is the ground speed and true heading?
If a pilot is on a true heading of 121°, with a ground speed of 140 kts, and the winds aloft are 330° at 40 kts, what is the true course? Additionally, what is the pilot's original true airspeed?

There is a missing boat off shore (heading 268° out and 88° in) and an aircraft has a total 5 hours and 30 minutes on board. The pilot is tasked with flying to a point 75 miles offshore to look for the missing boat and return with at least 45 minutes of fuel onboard. If the TAS is 105 kts and the winds aloft are 240° at 21 kts, how much time can the pilot spend on station searching for the boat?
Additionally, the actual winds aloft are discovered to be 210° at 39 kts, how much time are you now able to spend searching for the missing boat?
questions.context: Referenced question 18: There is a missing boat off shore (heading 268° out and 88° in) and an aircraft has a total 5 hours and 30 minutes on board. The pilot is tasked with flying to a point 75 miles offshore to look for the missing boat and return with at least 45 minutes of fuel onboard. If the TAS is 105 kts and the winds aloft are 240° at 21 kts, how much time can the pilot spend on station searching for the boat? [[DIVIDER]] 3 (Referring to Question #18) As you are flying out to the search area, new information is relayed stating the boat is suspected of drifting an additional 10 miles offshore.

questions.question: Performing a maintenance flight, a pilot has to fly for 4 hours and 20 minutes in cruise flight. If the winds aloft are 240° at 19 kts, the true course out is 021°, and the TAS in cruise is 145 kts, what is the time to turn back and what is that distance out?
If two airports A and B are 212 NM, what is this distance in statute miles and kilometers?
Seattle's new runway (16R / 34L) is exactly 8500 feet long, how long is that in meters?

Passing by the Manchester, NH airport (KMHT), a pilot wishes to check his/her ground speed using the VOR. If the pilot is 54 NM from the VOR and it takes 4 minutes 24 seconds to travel between the 180° and 168° radials, what is the ground speed?
A 747-400 is checking in on a center frequency at 40,000 feet at Mach 0.77. If the 747 is in a standard atmosphere, what is their TAS?

4 If the CG range of an aircraft is 81.2 to 89.2, with a weight of 2700 lbs, and the current CG is at 91.7, what is the minimum weight that needs to be moved from the aft cargo area (Station 120) to the forward cargo area (Station 25) to bring the aircraft within limits?

It takes 1 hour and 23 minutes to travel 141 NM with a True Course of 125°, TAS of 110 kts, and True Heading of 121°. How long will it take to return to the original destination? __________________ minutes

An aircraft is loaded as such: Empty Weight 2300 lbs at 75.0" 2 Forward Passengers (150 lbs each) 300 lbs at 70.0" 2 Aft Passengers (170 lbs each) 340 lbs at 95.0" 40 US Gallons of AVGAS 240 lbs at 80.0" 50 lbs of cargo 50 lbs at 125.0" What is the weight and balance of this loaded aircraft?
(Referring to Question #27) If an aft passenger (170 lbs) decides they do not want to go, how much and in what direction does the CG shift?
questions.context: Referenced question 27: An aircraft is loaded as such: Empty Weight 2300 lbs at 75.0" 2 Forward Passengers (150 lbs each) 300 lbs at 70.0" 2 Aft Passengers (170 lbs each) 340 lbs at 95.0" 40 US Gallons of AVGAS 240 lbs at 80.0" 50 lbs of cargo 50 lbs at 125.0" What is the weight and balance of this loaded aircraft?

questions.question: After traveling 96 NM, the aircraft is 6.3 NM left of course. What is the total correction needed to return on course in 20 NM?

5 While flying nearing Knoxville, TN, you fly past the Volunteer VOR. After passing the Volunteer VOR (KVXV) radials 116° to 134° in 5.1 minutes, the pilot calculates a ground speed of 133 kts. How far from the VOR did the aircraft pass?

An aircraft (Empty Weight and CG of 1950 lbs at 65.0") has a CG limit between 67.5" and 73.0" and a maximum takeoff weight of 2850 lbs. The pilot (170 lbs), Tom (180 lbs), Samantha (120 lbs), and Laura (110 lbs) are flying to Orlando with 50 US Gallons of AVGAS (at 80.0"). The pilot and Samantha are up front (at 55.0") and Tom and Laura are in back (at 90.0"). Can all the passengers go to Orlando within legal limits?

choices.text: Yes, by only 20 lbs and just barely inside the CG limit
Yes, by 120 lbs and well with CG limit
No, outside of the CG envelope

questions.correctChoice: Yes, by only 20 lbs and just barely inside the CG limit

questions.question: At a pressure altitude of 24,000 feet with an OAT of -14°C and a calibrated altitude of 24,000 feet, what is the true altitude?
At takeoff from an airport (8820 ft MSL), with an altimeter of 29.63", and a temperature of 91°F, what is the true altitude?
(Referring to Question #33) If the IAS is 470 km/hr, what is the TAS and Mach value?
questions.context: Referenced question 33: At takeoff from an airport (8820 ft MSL), with an altimeter of 29.63", and a temperature of 91°F, what is the true altitude?

questions.question: At roughly 6000 inches, the Airbus 380 is how many statute miles and how meters long?

A pilot is departing Ketchikan, AK (30 feet above sea level) on a true course of 196°, where the altimeter setting is 29.92". There is a hill 6.2 NM away that is 2607 feet above field elevation. If the winds aloft are 230° at 41 kts, and OAT of +30°C, and the indicated airspeed is 120 kts in the climb, what rate of climb is required to clear the obstacle by at least 100 feet?
Flying at an indicated altitude of 9500 feet, the OAT is -12°C with an altimeter setting of 30.13". What is the true altitude?

On a flight between two airports 200 NM apart and on a heading of 120°, there is a ridgeline exactly halfway between the two airports, which is perpendicular to the course. The pilot wishes to deviate to the left at a 45° angle, to intercept the ridgeline. The pilot wishes to deviate 13.3 NM before the ridgeline. Assuming no wind. If the pilot then wishes to re- intercept the course 50 NM immediately after the ridgeline, what is the new heading to intercept the original course?
195 kg of oil are equal to how many pounds of AVGAS? ___________________________________ pounds of AVGAS (whole number of pounds)
